# AXPONA — Strategic DDD Architecture

> **Phase:** Steps 1-2 of the [SemOps Engagement Lifecycle](https://github.com/timjmitchell/research-pr/blob/main/docs/research/engagement-lifecycle.md)
> **Engagement:** [axpona-pr#3](https://github.com/timjmitchell/axpona-pr/issues/3)
> **Source:** Pipeline-derived from classification.yaml + business-model.yaml + pre-pipeline 1P artifacts
> **Method:** BIZBOK → DDD derivation (anchor noun rule)
> **Classification:** [classification.yaml](classification.yaml) | **Business Model:** [business-model.yaml](business-model.yaml)
>
> **See Also:** [UBIQUITOUS_LANGUAGE.md](UBIQUITOUS_LANGUAGE.md) | [system-inventory.yaml](system-inventory.yaml) | [system-classification.yaml](system-classification.yaml)

## Architecture Overview

8 bounded contexts — 4 CORE, 2 SUPPORTING, 2 GENERIC — modeling a two-sided physical marketplace for consumer audio trade shows. Work-dominant system mix (app 20%, analytics 15%, work 40%, record 25%).

**Revenue:** $2.1M (2025), $780K EBITDA (36% margin), 3 direct staff
**Hard problem:** Venue capacity ceiling (213 listening rooms) + 3-person team concentration risk
**Moat:** Niche monopoly, listening room format innovation, 24-year relationship capital

---

| 6 | **Interaction** | Interaction | SUPPORTING | Application | Interaction | 3p_baseline |
| 7 | **Agreement** | Agreement | GENERIC | Record | Agreement | 3p_baseline |
| 8 | **Finance** | FinancialAccount | GENERIC | Record | FinancialAccount | 3p_baseline |

## Bounded Context Detail

### 1. Exhibitor — Marketplace Supply Side (CORE)

**Anchor noun:** Exhibitor
**Domain type:** CORE — 74% of revenue (listening rooms + booths). Rebooking rate (80%) is the leading indicator. 2 of 3 staff are exhibitor sales.
**System type:** Work — sales pipeline, vendor coordination, relationship management
**Signal source:** 3p_baseline
**Pattern:** build_custom
**Current system:** CRM (likely Salesforce/HubSpot/spreadsheet) — unconfirmed

**Aggregate root:** `Exhibitor`
- **Entities:** ExhibitorProfile, RebookingRecord, ProductListing, ServiceOrder, LeadRetrievalLicense
- **Value Objects:** PriorityPoints {points, earnedFromEditionId}, ExhibitorPackage {space, services[], sponsorshipAddOns[], totalPrice}, ContactInfo {name, email, phone, company}
- **States:** Prospected → Applied → Contracted → Confirmed → Attended → Rebooking

**Commands:**
- `OnboardExhibitor` — Create exhibitor record from application, assign sales owner
- `AssignSpace` — Link exhibitor to exhibition space, generate agreement
- `ProcessRebooking` — Accept rebooking within 30-day window, reserve space, update priority points
- `FulfillServiceOrder` — Complete exhibitor service request (drayage, AV, electrical)
- `ActivateLeadRetrieval` — Provision lead retrieval license for exhibitor

**Domain Events:**
- `ExhibitorContracted` — Agreement signed, space reserved → consumed by Exhibition Space, Agreement, Finance
- `ExhibitorRebooked` — 30-day window rebooking accepted → consumed by Exhibition Space (next edition), Finance
- `ServiceOrderFulfilled` — Exhibitor service completed → consumed by Finance

**Key metrics:** Rebooking rate (80%), net exhibitor growth, average contract value, services attach rate

---

### 2. Sponsorship — Advertising/Media (CORE)

**Anchor noun:** Sponsorship
**Domain type:** CORE — fastest-growing line (4.3x since 2018), demand exceeds supply, pricing power untapped
**System type:** Work — tracking, deliverable coordination
**Signal source:** 1p_refinement (promoted from SUPPORTING based on growth trajectory)
**Pattern:** build_custom
**Current system:** Spreadsheet tracking (unconfirmed)

**Aggregate root:** `Sponsorship`
- **Entities:** SponsorshipDeliverable, SponsorshipItem
- **Value Objects:** SponsorshipPackage {tier, price, deliverables[], exclusivityFlag}, MonetaryAmount {amount, currency}
- **States:** Proposed → Sold → Contracted → Active → Delivered → Reported

**Commands:**
- `SellSponsorship` — Create sponsorship from inventory item, assign to sponsor
- `ActivateSponsorshipDeliverable` — Begin production/placement of sponsorship deliverable
- `VerifyDelivery` — Confirm deliverable was placed/executed as contracted

**Domain Events:**
- `SponsorshipSold` — Inventory item committed → consumed by Finance, Agreement
- `SponsorshipDelivered` — All deliverables verified → consumed by Finance (final billing)

**Key metrics:** Fill rate (>90% estimated), CPM equivalent ($19/1K attendees), yield, renewal rate

---

### 3. Exhibition Space (CORE)

**Anchor noun:** ExhibitionSpace
**Domain type:** CORE — listening room format IS the product. Revenue per sqft ($110/sqft) is the value measure. Venue capacity ceiling is the binding constraint.
**System type:** Work — floor plan management, assignment workflow
**Signal source:** 1p_refinement (weight increased — listening rooms generate 65% of revenue)
**Pattern:** build_custom
**Current system:** Floor plan tool (manual or specialized) — unconfirmed

**Aggregate root:** `ExhibitionSpace`
- **Entities:** SpaceAssignment, FloorPlan, Venue
- **Value Objects:** SpaceDimensions {width, depth, sqft, cornerFlag, electricalAccess}, SpaceType {listening_room, expo, ear_gear, car_audio, alti}
- **States:** Planned → Available → Reserved → Assigned → Occupied → Vacated

**Commands:**
- `PublishFloorPlan` — Release floor plan version with available spaces
- `ReserveSpace` — Hold space for exhibitor (pre-assignment)
- `AssignSpace` — Confirm exhibitor-to-space assignment
- `VacateSpace` — Release space post-show for next edition planning

**Domain Events:**
- `FloorPlanPublished` — New floor plan version released → consumed by Exhibitor (sales team), Event
- `SpaceAssigned` — Exhibitor confirmed in space → consumed by Exhibitor, Agreement
- `SpaceVacated` — Post-show space release → consumed by Event (next edition planning)

**Space types:** listening_room ($5,250 avg), expo ($1,300–$3,500), ear_gear ($1,500–$2,500), car_audio (varies), alti (premium)

**Key metrics:** Floor plan fill %, revenue per sqft ($110), listening room premium ratio

---

### 4. Event — Edition Lifecycle (CORE)

**Anchor noun:** Event
**Domain type:** CORE — each edition is a discrete project with its own P&L ($2.1M revenue, $780K EBITDA)
**System type:** Work — project management, logistics coordination
**Signal source:** 3p_baseline
**Pattern:** build_custom
**Current system:** Internal event management — unconfirmed

**Aggregate root:** `Event`
- **Entities:** EventEdition, Session, Speaker
- **Value Objects:** EditionDateRange {startDate, endDate, setupDays}, ProgramSlot {time, room, title, speaker}
- **States:** Planning → Open → Live → Post-Show → Archived

**Commands:**
- `PlanEdition` — Create next edition, set dates, open venue coordination
- `OpenRegistration` — Make edition available for exhibitor and attendee registration
- `ExecuteShow` — Transition to live state, begin on-site operations
- `CloseEdition` — Archive edition, trigger post-show reporting and rebooking window

**Domain Events:**
- `EditionPlanned` — Next edition created → consumed by Exhibition Space (floor plan), Exhibitor (sales), Attendee (registration)
- `EventOpenedForRegistration` — Registration opens → consumed by Attendee, Exhibitor
- `EventConcluded` — Show ends → consumed by Exhibitor (rebooking window opens), Interaction (lead delivery), Finance (final reconciliation)

**Key metrics:** Edition margin (36% EBITDA), YoY revenue growth (15%), on-time delivery

---

### 5. Attendee — Marketplace Demand Side (SUPPORTING)

**Anchor noun:** Attendee
**Domain type:** SUPPORTING — 13% of revenue. External vendor handles operations. Growth via market expansion, not retention.
**System type:** Application — registration system, badge management
**Signal source:** 3p_baseline
**Pattern:** buy_adapt
**Current system:** Unity Event Solutions (confirmed — external ticketing vendor)

**Aggregate root:** `Attendee`
- **Entities:** Registration, Badge
- **Value Objects:** Ticket {ticketType, price, accessLevel}, DemographicProfile {geography, industry, interests}
- **States:** Registered → Ticketed → Checked-In → No-Show

**Commands:**
- `RegisterAttendee` — Create registration, assign ticket type
- `CheckInAttendee` — Badge scan at entrance, activate for lead capture

**Domain Events:**
- `AttendeeRegistered` — Registration completed → consumed by Finance (ticket revenue)
- `AttendeeCheckedIn` — On-site arrival → consumed by Interaction (enable lead capture)

**Ticket types:** general_admission ($32–$40), friday/saturday/sunday_pass ($15–$40), vip ($100–$150), trade_pass (varies)

**Key metrics:** Attendee-to-exhibitor ratio (11.5:1), ARPU ($32), check-in rate

---

### 6. Interaction — Lead Capture (SUPPORTING)

**Anchor noun:** Interaction
**Domain type:** SUPPORTING — service offering to exhibitors, vendor-provided hardware/software
**System type:** Application — badge scanning, real-time capture
**Signal source:** 3p_baseline
**Pattern:** buy_adapt
**Current system:** Badge scanning vendor (unconfirmed)

**Aggregate root:** `Interaction`
- **Entities:** LeadRecord
- **Value Objects:** ScanTimestamp {datetime, location}
- **States:** Planned → In-Progress → Past

**Commands:**
- `CaptureLeadScan` — Record badge scan event between exhibitor and attendee
- `DeliverLeadReport` — Package lead data for exhibitor post-show

**Domain Events:**
- `LeadsCaptured` — Batch of leads recorded → consumed by Exhibitor (ROI reporting)
- `LeadReportDelivered` — Exhibitor receives lead summary → consumed by Exhibitor

**Note:** MatchmakingAppointment dropped from hand-built model — gap analysis found it not observable and likely not implemented.

**Key metrics:** Leads per exhibitor, lead-to-conversion rate

---

### 7. Agreement (GENERIC)

**Anchor noun:** Agreement
**Domain type:** GENERIC — standard contract management
**System type:** Record — authoritative legal facts
**Signal source:** 3p_baseline
**Pattern:** buy_commodity
**Current system:** DocuSign/PandaDoc equivalent (unconfirmed)

**Aggregate root:** `Agreement`
- **Entities:** AgreementTerm
- **Value Objects:** PaymentSchedule {installments[], dueDate}, TermsAndConditions
- **States:** Pending → In Force → Terminated → Abandoned

**Commands:**
- `ActivateAgreement` — Finalize and activate exhibitor/sponsor contract

**Domain Events:**
- `AgreementActivated` — Contract in force → consumed by Exhibitor, Sponsorship, Finance

**Integration pattern:** Published Language — Agreement exposes canonical contract schema consumed by multiple contexts.

---

### 8. Finance (GENERIC)

**Anchor noun:** FinancialAccount
**Domain type:** GENERIC — standard payment and accounting
**System type:** Record — authoritative financial facts
**Signal source:** 3p_baseline
**Pattern:** buy_commodity
**Current system:** QuickBooks/equivalent + payment processing (unconfirmed)

**Aggregate root:** `FinancialAccount`
- **Entities:** FinancialTransaction, Payment, Invoice
- **Value Objects:** MonetaryAmount {amount, currency}, RevenueAllocation {editionId, category, amount}
- **States (Payment):** Unpaid → Paid → Settled | Cancelled → Reversed

**Commands:**
- `AllocateRevenue` — Map payment to edition + revenue category (space, sponsorship, ticket, services)

**Domain Events:**
- `PaymentReceived` — Cash collected → consumed by Exhibitor (account status), Event (edition P&L)

### Context Map Relationships

| Upstream | Downstream | Pattern | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|---------|-----------|
| Exhibitor | Exhibition Space | Customer-Supplier | Exhibitor consumes space data via ID join |
| Exhibition Space | Event | Shared Kernel | `dim_edition` is a conformed dimension shared by both |
| Event | Attendee | Conformist | Attendee adopts `edition_id` from Event directly |
| Attendee | Finance | ACL | Revenue allocation translates ticket purchases to financial line items |
| Agreement | Exhibitor, Sponsorship, Finance | Published Language | Canonical contract schema consumed by all |
| Sponsorship | Interaction | Customer-Supplier | Sponsorship consumes interaction metrics for ROI reporting |
| Interaction | Exhibitor | Customer-Supplier | Lead data delivered to exhibitors post-show |
| Event | Exhibitor | Customer-Supplier | Edition lifecycle triggers exhibitor sales cycle |

---

## Sagas

### Edition Lifecycle Saga (Primary)

**Trigger:** `EditionPlanned`
**Participants:** Event, Exhibition Space, Exhibitor, Sponsorship, Attendee, Interaction, Agreement, Finance
**Duration:** ~12 months (annual cycle)

**Steps:**
1. **Plan** — Event creates edition → Exhibition Space publishes floor plan → Exhibitor sales opens
2. **Sell** — Exhibitor contracts space → Agreement activates → Finance invoices → Sponsorship sells inventory
3. **Market** — Event opens registration → Attendee registers → Finance processes tickets
4. **Execute** — Event goes live → Interaction captures leads → Attendee checks in
5. **Retain** — Event concludes → Exhibitor rebooking window opens (30 days) → Interaction delivers lead reports → Finance reconciles edition P&L

**Compensation:**
- `ExhibitorCancelled` → Exhibition Space releases assignment, Finance processes refund/forfeiture
- `VenueUnavailable` → Event reschedules, all contexts notified
- `EditionCancelled` → Full unwind (COVID precedent: 2020, 2021)

### Sponsorship Fulfillment Saga

**Trigger:** `SponsorshipSold`
**Participants:** Sponsorship, Agreement, Finance, Event
**Duration:** Edition-bound (sale through delivery verification)

**Steps:**
1. Sponsorship sold → Agreement activated → Finance invoices
2. Event goes live → Sponsorship deliverables activated (placement, signage, branding)
3. Event concludes → Sponsorship deliverables verified → Finance final billing
4. Post-show → Sponsorship renewal opportunity for next edition

**Compensation:**
- `DeliverableFailed` → Sponsorship offers substitution or credit
- `SponsorCancelled` → Finance processes refund, inventory returned to available

---

## System Inventory Mapping

| Context | Confirmed System | Gaps |
|---------|-----------------|------|
| Exhibitor | CRM (unconfirmed — likely spreadsheet) | No formal pipeline tracking |
| Sponsorship | Spreadsheet (unconfirmed) | No inventory management system |
| Exhibition Space | Manual floor plan (unconfirmed) | No optimization tooling |
| Event | Internal management (unconfirmed) | No project management platform confirmed |
| Attendee | Unity Event Solutions (confirmed) | — |
| Interaction | Badge scanning vendor (unconfirmed) | Lead delivery process unconfirmed |
| Agreement | Document management (unconfirmed) | — |
| Finance | QuickBooks/equivalent (unconfirmed) | Revenue allocation likely manual |

**Key gap:** CRM and data tooling are the most important unconfirmed systems. Whether exhibitor relationships are managed in Salesforce, HubSpot, or spreadsheets determines operational maturity.

---

## The Conformed Dimension: Edition

The **edition is the join key** across all contexts — the trade show equivalent of a SaaS monthly cohort. Every fact table, every metric, every cross-context analysis is organized by edition.

## Leading vs Lagging Indicators

| Leading (predictive) | Lagging (confirmatory) |
|---|---|
| Exhibitor rebooking rate (30 days post-show) | Next-year booth revenue |
| Sponsorship renewal rate | Next-year sponsorship revenue |
| Waitlist depth | Pricing power |
| Early-bird ticket velocity | Total attendance |
| Floor plan fill % at T-90 | Show quality |

**The single most important metric:** Exhibitor rebooking rate within 30 days of show close. Confirmed at **80%**. If this drops, everything else follows.

## Principles

1. **Partners fund the ecosystem.** Exhibitors and sponsors contribute ~82% of revenue.
2. **Edition is the anchor.** Every analysis is organized by edition — the conformed dimension.
3. **Rebooking rate is the canary.** The 30-day post-show rate predicts all revenue streams.
4. **Work systems dominate.** At 3 staff and <$5M, most "systems" are spreadsheets and email.
5. **Listening rooms are domain-specific.** Private demo spaces are unique to audio — AXPONA's format innovation.
6. **Venue capacity is the binding constraint.** 213 rooms at the Renaissance Schaumburg. Growth comes from pricing power and space type innovation, not capacity.
